shortDescription.text: Open redirect via unvalidated 'next' URL parameter

fullDescription.text: The endpoint reads a 'next' URL parameter from the query string and passes it directly to Flask's redirect() function without validating the destination host or scheme. An attacker can craft a URL like /api/redirect?next=https://evil.com that redirects authenticated users to a phishing site, leveraging the trusted domain to bypass user suspicion.

message.text: Open redirect: The 'next' query parameter from request.args is passed directly to Flask's redirect() without validating the destination host or scheme. An attacker can craft a link such as /api/redirect?next=https://evil.com/login to redirect users to a phishing page that mimics the legitimate application. Because the redirect originates from a trusted domain, users are unlikely to notice. This enables credential harvesting, OAuth token theft (if used in auth flows), and session fixation attacks.

par-analysis.principal: External HTTP client (unauthenticated GET request with attacker-controlled 'next' parameter)
par-analysis.action: No URL host/scheme validation, no allowlist of permitted redirect targets, no same-origin check
par-analysis.resource: Flask redirect() — HTTP 302 response to attacker-controlled URL
